2604.12748.title: Generating Effective CoT Traces for Mitigating Causal Hallucination
2604.12748.paper_summary: Although large language models (LLMs) excel in complex reasoning tasks, they suffer from severe causal hallucination in event causality identification (ECI), particularly in smaller models ($\leq$1.5B parameters). A promising approach to address this issue is to fine-tune them with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) traces. However, there is currently a lack of CoT trace dataset available for ECI. In this paper, we first investigate the essential criteria that effective CoT traces should possess to mitigate causal hallucination in smaller models. We then design a pipeline to generate CoT traces that meet these criteria. Moreover, since there is currently no metric for quantifying causal hallucination, we also introduce a new metric, the Causal Hallucination Rate (CHR), to quantify causal hallucination, guide the formulation of effective CoT trace criteria, and validate the effectiveness of our pipeline. Our experiments show that fine-tuning with the CoT traces generated by our pipeline not only substantially reduces causal hallucination in smaller LLMs but also improves mean accuracy. Moreover, the fine-tuned models exhibit strong cross-dataset and cross-difficulty generalization, as well as robustness under misleading intervention prompts.
2604.12748.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在複雜推理任務中表現優異，但在事件因果識別（ECI）中，它們遭受嚴重的因果幻覺，尤其是在較小的模型（$\leq$1.5B 參數）中。解決這一問題的一個有前景的方法是使用思維鏈（CoT）痕跡對其進行微調。然而，目前缺乏可用於 ECI 的 CoT 痕跡數據集。在本文中，我們首先調查有效的 CoT 痕跡應具備的基本標準，以減輕較小模型中的因果幻覺。然後，我們設計了一個管道來生成符合這些標準的 CoT 痕跡。此外，由於目前沒有量化因果幻覺的度量標準，我們還介紹了一個新的度量標準，即因果幻覺率（CHR），用於量化因果幻覺，指導有效的 CoT 痕跡標準的制定，並驗證我們管道的有效性。我們的實驗表明，使用我們管道生成的 CoT 痕跡進行微調不僅顯著減少了較小 LLM 中的因果幻覺，還提高了平均準確率。此外，微調後的模型在跨數據集和跨難度的泛化能力上表現強勁，並且在誤導性干預提示下展現出穩健性。
2604.12748.author: Yiheng Zhao et.al.
